kept.text:
$RKLB 
The undisputed backbone of the small-launch economy. RKLB posted record Q4 revenue of ~$180M, up 36% YoY, with backlog surging 73% to $1.85B.  CEO Peter Beck cut his salary to $1 and forfeited RSUs — signaling full conviction in shareholder upside.  RKLB completed two launches within 48 hours from separate sites , and filed a Neutron rocket launch permit for a July–December 2026 debut window  — the biggest upcoming catalyst. Q1 2026 guidance calls for $185M–$200M in revenue, implying ~57% YoY growth at the midpoint.  The FCC spectrum reform and SpaceX IPO halo are tailwinds — RKLB has the infrastructure to capitalize on both. Not financial advice.

tweet.text:
My top 5 space/defense companies to own for the next decade: 

1) $RKLB - End to end space company encompassing launch, payloads, radio, solar, and infrastructure builds. Has transitioned from a small rocket company into a tier-one defense prime contractor with involvement in the SDA Tranche 3, hypersonics and Golden Dome. Their Geost, SolAero and the yet to be closed Mynaric aquisition complete the 'innards" of building defense satellites in-house. The upcoming Neutron rocket was specifically designed to meet the requirements of the U.S. Space Force's NSSL Phase Lane 1 program which allows them to compete directly dor the most sensitive and lucrative "national security" launches. ✅  

2) $ASTS - First space based mobile broadband network with applications across 5G, radar, sensing, IoT, navigation, defense. Resilient SATCOM, Blue Force Tracking, Emergency Response. The launch of AST SpaceMobile USA which is specifically structured to meet the security clearances and regulatory requirements of the DoD. Recently awarded a $30M SDA prime contract under the HALO Europa program specifically for AST's salellites to communicate directly to unmodified military radios. Only one of 2 companies in the US with full commercial authorization for D2D space based broadband satellite. ✅ 

3) $PL -  Designs, builds and operates one of the largest fleets of Earth observation satellites serving defence, AI, gov’t, energy, tech sectors. Planet Labs has recently transitioned from a high-growth "SPAC-era" story into a fundamentally stronger, profitable infrastructure play. As of late March 2026, the company is being referred to by analysts as the "Bloomberg of Earth Data" due to its massive, searchable archive of the planet’s surface. ✅

4) $MDA -  Provides the infrastructure/expertise for space exploration and satellite communications. Serves nearly every sector of the expanding space economy. In 2025, MDA launched 49th North, a dedicated defense business unit. This positions the company to capture the surge in "dual-use" space spending (missions that serve both civilian and military purposes), particularly in missile defense and secure sovereign communications. ✅      

5) $TER - Designs, develops and manufactures automated test equipment and advanced robotics systems. It is also now the critical quality gate for the AI hardware explosion. ✅
